runs.text:
- Customer may cancel the purchase contract within 3 business days for a full refund; after this period, the deposit is forfeited [1].
- The PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT may be terminated by either party upon sixty (60) days written notice [2].
- In the event the PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT is terminated, or the property is sold, the Owner must honor all confirmed reservations [5].
- Termination of the PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT does not invalidate any existing rental agreements made by Agent on Owner's behalf; Owner remains responsible for obligations or expenses incurred by Agent prior to termination [7].
- The BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY terminates one (1) year or sixty (60) days after commencement, as applicable, unless otherwise provided [3].
- If the first purchaser sells the home or moves out, the BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY automatically terminates [4].
- The HOLDING TANK SERVICING CONTRACT remains in effect until the owner or pumper terminates the contract; upon any change, the owner must file a copy of changes or a new contract with the municipality and County within ten (10) business days from the date of change [6].

questions.query: Identify which documents reference **jurisdictions / governing law**.

runs.text:
- The purchase contract shall be governed by the laws of the State of Florida [1].
- The PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT shall be governed by the laws of the State of Hawaii [2].
- The PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT references an insured financial institution authorized to do business in the State of Hawaii [17].
- The BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY states that disputes shall be resolved in accordance with the applicable substantive law of the State of Idaho [3].
- The BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY references proceedings in any court having jurisdiction [4].
- The BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY specifies arbitrations shall be conducted in Pocatello, Idaho [5].
- The BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY references the Small Claims Court of the State of Idaho [6].
- The BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY references the venue and jurisdiction of the Small Claims Department of the Magistrate Division of the District Courts of the State of Idaho [7].
- The BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY references claims within the jurisdiction limits of the small claims court of the State of Idaho [8].
- The BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY references a court having jurisdiction for replevin, garnishment, or appointment of a receiver [9].
- The BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY references a court of competent jurisdiction for implied warranties [10].
- The HOLDING TANK SERVICING CONTRACT requires filing with the County of Washburn, as required under WI Code SPS 383.21(2)5 [12].

questions.query: Summarize "who pays what" across the set (fees/charges/taxes).

runs.text:
- Owner pays a non-refundable start-up fee of $250.00 to the Agent upon execution of the Property Management Agreement [3].
- Owner pays a $50.00/month minimum charge for administration and accounting (excluding tax preparation/returns/forms) [7].
- Owner pays a fee/commission of 10% of gross revenues for management services for long-term leases (leases of more than 180 days) [9].
- Owner pays a fee/commission of 25% of gross revenues for management services for short-term and/or vacation rentals (reservations of less than 180 days) [10].
- Owner pays a pro-ration charge for advertising for short-term (vacation rentals) at $75.00/month [9].
- Owner pays a fee of 10% on the total charge for repairs, upgrades, and/or renovations [7].
- Owner pays $35/hour for scheduling repairs, upgrades, renovations, replacement of appliances, carpets, curtains, drapes, and/or to escort contractors, repair persons, technicians, and handymen [1].
- Owner pays Hawaii Gross Excise tax (currently 4.712%) on all fees, commissions, and charges assessed by the Agent [1].
- Owner pays credit card and/or bankcard fees, which the Agent may deduct from the Owner's proceeds [10].
- Owner pays for charges incurred for media invoices, which are passed on to the Owner on the monthly proceeds statements [4].
- Owner pays for housekeeping and regular maintenance services contracted and authorized by the Agent on the Owner's behalf [19].
- Owner pays the pumper for charges incurred in servicing the holding tank(s) as mutually agreed upon by the owner and pumper [8].
- Each party pays one-half (1/2) of the cost and fees of arbitration and their respective costs, including attorneys' fees, in any arbitration under the Builders Limited Warranty [2].
- Any party who fails to submit to binding arbitration bears all costs and expenses, including reasonable attorneys' fees, incurred by the other party in compelling arbitration [11].
- Customer pays the total contract price of $29,900.00 in three installments: $20,000.00 upon signing, $7,000.00 upon delivery, $2,900.00 upon completion [5].
- Customer bears any additional compliance costs under the purchase contract [6].
- Legal fees are recoverable by the Contractor under the purchase contract [14].
- Customer pays invoice amounts to Contoso Lifts LLC as stated (e.g., $29,900.00 for two Hall Call stations) [12][15][16][17].

runs.text:
- Provide Owner with a monthly statement of income and expenses for the property [1].
- Complete an initial property inspection and prepare a condition report of the unit [3].
- Conduct a complete inventory of all furniture and furnishings in the rental unit, and produce a condition/inspection report of the premises, appliances, and contents [6].
- Provide the invoice(s) from the media, and pass on the cost to the Owner on the monthly proceeds statements [8].
- Owner to provide Agent with written notification within five (5) business days of a listing agreement if the property is for sale [13].
- Owner to provide Agent with Owner's General Excise Tax License [16].
- Owner to provide Agent with Owner's U.S. Federal Tax Id, or Social Security number [16].
- Owner to furnish Agent with a copy of the insurance policy and a certificate of insurance [21].
- The pumper agrees to submit to the County, reports for the servicing of the holding tank(s) in manner specified by the County, including: name and address of the person responsible for servicing the holding tank [2].
- The owner agrees to file a copy of this contract with the County of Washburn [4].
- The owner agrees to file a copy of any changes to this service contract or a copy of a new service contract with the municipality and the County within ten (10) business days from the date of change [5].
- The pumper's report must include: the sanitary permit number issued for the holding tank; the dates on which the holding tank was serviced [14].
- The pumper's report must include: the volumes in gallons of the contents pumped from the holding tank for each servicing; the disposal sites to which the contents from the holding tank were delivered [15].
- The pumper's report must include: condition of holding tank and components at time of inspection [18].
- Buyer/Owner must write a letter describing a defect to the Builder and send it by certified mail return receipt requested [7].
- Only emergency reports will be taken by phone [9].
- Buyer/Owner must tell the Builder in writing what times during the day they will be at home for scheduling service calls [11].

questions.query: What remedies / dispute-resolution mechanisms are described?

runs.text:
- Binding arbitration is required for all disputes except those specifically preserved, such as small claims court actions within the jurisdiction limits of the State of Idaho [1], [4], [6].
- Claims for money not exceeding $3,000 may be pursued in the Small Claims Department of the Magistrate Division of the District Courts of the State of Idaho [12].
- Parties may pursue claims of lien or foreclose a lien or mortgage against real or personal property collateral or security, as necessary to preserve such remedy or to enforce any arbitration award, or obtain a personal or deficiency award [2].
- Parties may exercise self-help remedies, including repossession and set-off rights [2].
- Parties may obtain provisional or ancillary remedies such as injunctive relief, sequestration, attachment, claim & delivery, replevin, garnishment, or the appointment of a receiver from a court having jurisdiction [3].
- The arbitrator(s) may consolidate multiple party claims that are substantially identical or based on the same underlying transaction, and may consolidate claims and disputes between other parties related to the subject matter, including claims by or against subcontractors or suppliers [7].
- Arbitration is to be administered by the American Arbitration Association (AAA) or another mutually agreeable administrator, following the AAA Building Arbitration Rules or other mutually agreed rules [8].
- The arbitrator(s) are empowered to resolve disputes regarding the terms of the arbitration clause, including questions about the arbitrability of any dispute, but cannot change or alter the terms of the Arbitration Program [9].
- The arbitrator(s) must resolve all disputes in accordance with the substantive law of the State of Idaho [10].
- The parties are to take action to conclude arbitration within 180 days of filing, and the arbitrator(s) may impose sanctions for failure to proceed within this timeframe [11].
- Each party is responsible for one-half of the cost and fees of arbitration and their own attorneys' fees; the arbitrator's award must be in writing and set forth the factual and legal basis for the award [13].
- Arbitration is to be conducted in Pocatello, Idaho [14].
- The builder will repair or replace covered defects at no charge to the buyer/owner within sixty (60) days, unless delayed by weather, labor, or material shortages [16].
- If the buyer/owner believes a defect is covered, they must write a letter describing the defect to the builder and send it by certified mail before the end of the one (1) year or sixty (60) day warranty period, as applicable [18].

full_response.response:
- Binding arbitration is required for all disputes except those specifically preserved, such as small claims court actions within the jurisdiction limits of the State of Idaho [1], [4], [6].
- Claims for money not exceeding $3,000 may be pursued in the Small Claims Department of the Magistrate Division of the District Courts of the State of Idaho [12].
- Parties may pursue claims of lien or foreclose a lien or mortgage against real or personal property collateral or security, as necessary to preserve such remedy or to enforce any arbitration award, or obtain a personal or deficiency award [2].
- Parties may exercise self-help remedies, including repossession and set-off rights [2].
- Parties may obtain provisional or ancillary remedies such as injunctive relief, sequestration, attachment, claim & delivery, replevin, garnishment, or the appointment of a receiver from a court having jurisdiction [3].
- The arbitrator(s) may consolidate multiple party claims that are substantially identical or based on the same underlying transaction, and may consolidate claims and disputes between other parties related to the subject matter, including claims by or against subcontractors or suppliers [7].
- Arbitration is to be administered by the American Arbitration Association (AAA) or another mutually agreeable administrator, following the AAA Building Arbitration Rules or other mutually agreed rules [8].
- The arbitrator(s) are empowered to resolve disputes regarding the terms of the arbitration clause, including questions about the arbitrability of any dispute, but cannot change or alter the terms of the Arbitration Program [9].
- The arbitrator(s) must resolve all disputes in accordance with the substantive law of the State of Idaho [10].
- The parties are to take action to conclude arbitration within 180 days of filing, and the arbitrator(s) may impose sanctions for failure to proceed within this timeframe [11].
- Each party is responsible for one-half of the cost and fees of arbitration and their own attorneys' fees; the arbitrator's award must be in writing and set forth the factual and legal basis for the award [13].
- Arbitration is to be conducted in Pocatello, Idaho [14].
- The builder will repair or replace covered defects at no charge to the buyer/owner within sixty (60) days, unless delayed by weather, labor, or material shortages [16].
- If the buyer/owner believes a defect is covered, they must write a letter describing the defect to the builder and send it by certified mail before the end of the one (1) year or sixty (60) day warranty period, as applicable [18].
